message:
test(repair): add proptest property-based tests for RaptorQ codec and LRC modules

10 new property-based tests:

codec (4 tests):
- encode_decode_roundtrip: random data with corruption <= repair budget
  always recovers exact original blocks
- encode_deterministic: same inputs produce identical repair symbols
- different_groups_different_seeds: distinct groups derive distinct seeds
- repair_count_matches_request: emitted symbol count matches requested

lrc (6 tests):
- local_parity_xor_identity: XOR of group data equals local parity
- local_repair_single_block: any single lost block recoverable via XOR
- config_total_blocks: data + local_parity + global_parity invariant
- global_repair_within_budget: GF(256) Gaussian elimination succeeds
- overhead_fraction_bounded: overhead always in (0, 1]
- config_serde_roundtrip: JSON serialization preserves all fields

Closes bd-14mr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-14jb.5","title":"Cross-crate integration: EBR GC coordinates with write-back cache eviction","description":"# Cross-crate integration: EBR GC coordinates with write-back cache eviction

## GOAL
Wire EBR-based version garbage collection to coordinate with the write-back cache eviction policy. When EBR GC reclaims old block versions, it must ensure no dirty cache entries reference those versions and that the cache eviction path respects epoch guards.

## BACKGROUND
EBR (bd-14jb) reclaims old MVCC versions. Write-back cache (bd-5s0r) holds dirty blocks. If GC reclaims a version that has dirty cache entries pending flush, data loss occurs. The cache eviction path must pin epochs during flush.

## DELIVERABLES
1. Cache eviction acquires epoch guard before flushing dirty blocks
2. GC skips versions with outstanding dirty cache references
3. Dirty flush completes before epoch can advance past that version
4. Integration test proving no use-after-free under concurrent GC + flush

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: epoch guard held during flush prevents GC of referenced version
2. Unit: GC correctly skips pinned versions
3. Stress: concurrent writers + GC + flush -- no data corruption (Miri-safe)
4. Integration: version reclaimed only after all dirty blocks flushed

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- GC skips pinned version (debug): version_id, pinned_by, epoch
- Flush acquires epoch guard (trace): flush_batch_id, epoch_id
- Flush releases epoch guard (trace): flush_batch_id, blocks_flushed
- GC reclaims version (info): version_id, freed_blocks, epoch_advanced_to

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. No use-after-free under concurrent GC + cache flush (Miri clean)
2. GC does not stall indefinitely (bounded epoch lag)
3.  {"id":"bd-14jb.6","title":"Add unit tests for EBR epoch advancement and version reclamation","description":"# Add unit tests for EBR epoch advancement and version reclamation

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the EBR for Block Version GC epic (bd-14jb). Currently has E2E test (bd-14jb.4) and benchmark (bd-14jb.3) but NO unit tests for epoch advancement, guard pinning, or version reclamation correctness.

## TEST PLAN

### Epoch Tests
1. Epoch advances when all guards dropped
2. Epoch does NOT advance while guard is held
3. Multiple concurrent guards: epoch waits for all
4. Guard drop order does not matter

### Reclamation Tests
5. Old version reclaimed after epoch advances past it
6. Version NOT reclaimed while any guard pins it
7. Reclamation batch processes multiple versions
8. Empty reclamation queue is no-op

### Version Chain Tests
9. Version chain length decreases after GC
10. Bounded chain length enforced (bd-14jb.2 integration)
11. Latest committed version never reclaimed
12. Aborted version reclaimed immediately

### Safety Tests
13. No use-after-free (Miri clean)
14. No memory leak under continuous write/GC cycle
15. Concurrent pin/unpin/reclaim does not corrupt state

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 15 tests above. Tests 13-14 require Miri.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log epoch state, guard count, reclamation queue

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 15 tests pass (including Miri)
2. No memory leaks verified via allocator stats
3.  {"id":"bd-14w","title":"ffs-types: Add missing canonical newtypes (BlockSize, ByteOffset, GroupNumber, DeviceId, Generation, ...)","description":"Goal: make ffs-types the single canonical home for cross-crate identifiers and basic arithmetic invariants.

Why: without strong newtypes we will silently mix units (bytes vs blocks, logical vs physical, ext4 group vs btrfs objectid) and ship corruption bugs.

Deliverables (minimum set):
- BlockSize(u32): must be power-of-two; ext4 v1 supports 1024/2048/4096 only.
- ByteOffset(u64): byte offsets on ByteDevice; checked add/sub/mul helpers.
- GroupNumber(u32): ext4 block group index.
- DeviceId(u64) or u128: stable id for multi-device future; for now single-device only.
- Generation(u64): btrfs generation / ext4 generation boundary type.
- LogicalBlock(BlockNumber) vs PhysicalBlock(BlockNumber) wrappers if we decide they are distinct.

Acceptance:
- All added newtypes have documented invariants + constructors that enforce them.
- Existing code in ffs-ondisk/ffs-block/ffs-core is updated to use these types where it improves correctness.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:19.015044302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:07:56.412273652Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:07:56.412252261Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}
 {"id":"bd-15c","title":"EPIC: Self-Healing Durability (RaptorQ)","description":"# EPIC: Self-Healing Durability (RaptorQ)

## PURPOSE
Implement FrankenFS’s second key innovation: automatic corruption recovery via fountain-coded repair symbols.

## CURRENT REALITY (CODE)
A meaningful portion of the foundation already exists:
- Codec core:
  - `crates/ffs-repair/src/codec.rs` implements deterministic group encode/decode via asupersync RaptorQ (SystematicEncoder + InactivationDecoder) with unit tests.
- Symbol layout:
  - `crates/ffs-repair/src/symbol.rs` defines a checksummed on-disk symbol header and group descriptor extension types.
- Scrub engine:
  - `crates/ffs-repair/src/scrub.rs` provides a generic block scrub pipeline that can surface corruption findings.

## WHAT’S STILL MISSING (THE REAL WORK)
1. Durable symbol storage I/O + reserved-space rules (so symbols exist after restart and are not allocated as user data).
2. Recovery orchestration (detect -> load symbols -> decode -> writeback -> verify) + evidence ledger.
3. End-to-end, reproducible corruption injection and recovery proof scripts.

## DESIGN REQUIREMENTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
Per AGENTS.md “alien artifact” bar for risky logic:
- Explicit invariants for recovery decisions.
- Evidence ledger (structured records) for every recovery attempt.
- No silent partial repair; fail loudly if redundancy insufficient.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (EPIC)
1. Repair symbols are generated and durably stored per block group (configurable overhead).
2. Controlled corruption triggers recovery attempts.
3. Successful recovery restores exact bytes and is verified.
4. Failures are graceful and explainable (clear error + evidence).
5. E2E script demonstrates bounded corruption + recovery deterministically with logs/artifacts.

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §4 (Self-Healing)
- `crates/ffs-repair/src/symbol.rs` (current storage model)

## Success Criteria
`scripts/e2e/ffs_repair_recovery_smoke.sh` passes and produces:
- corruption plan
- before/after checksums
- recovery evidence ledger
- clear PASS/FAIL summary","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:00.315447377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T08:52:11.045591197Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T08:52:11.045567222Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete; self-healing durability epic criteria met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["repair","semantics"],"comments":[{"id":117,"issue_id":"bd-15c","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: repair_durability
Mapped graveyard sections: 1.1 RaptorQ + 12.13 Change-point + 11.11 PoR
EV score: 25 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: decode_budget_ms=200, io_budget_mb=64, max_repair_attempts=3
Fallback trigger: read-only degrade + evidence ledger + manual repair workflow

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:18Z"},{"id":234,"issue_id":"bd-15c","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"All parent-child beads under bd-15c are now closed after completing bd-15c.10 daemon queued-refresh integration + tests. Epic acceptance criteria appear satisfied by existing pipeline/evidence/E2E coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-18T08:52:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15c.1","title":"Integrate asupersync RaptorQ codec into ffs-repair","description":"# Integrate asupersync RaptorQ codec into ffs-repair

## GOAL
Provide a deterministic, well-tested RaptorQ encode/decode core that FrankenFS can build storage + recovery workflows on top of.

## CURRENT REALITY (CODE)
This bead is **already implemented** in:
- `crates/ffs-repair/src/codec.rs`
  - `encode_group()` (SystematicEncoder)
  - `decode_group()` (InactivationDecoder)
  - `EncodedGroup` / `DecodeOutcome` types
- `crates/ffs-repair/src/symbol.rs`
  - deterministic seed derivation (`repair_seed`)

The unit test suite in `crates/ffs-repair/src/codec.rs` covers:
- determinism (same inputs/seed -> same symbols)
- successful recovery of 1+ corrupt blocks with sufficient repair symbols
- failure modes with insufficient symbols
- non-zero `first_block` offsets
- progressive fault injection

## OUT OF SCOPE (HANDLED BY OTHER BEADS)
- Persistent storage of repair symbols on disk (`bd-15c.2`).
- Recovery orchestration + evidence ledger (`bd-15c.3`).
- End-to-end corruption injection scripts (`bd-15c.4`).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `cargo test -p ffs-repair` passes.
2. Codec unit tests provide actionable failures (seed/K/block_size printed or derivable).
3. Public APIs are stable enough for storage/recovery layers to build on.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:17.711178940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T21:55:24.576465272Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T21:55:24.576447829Z","close_reason":"Implemented: crates/ffs-repair/src/codec.rs + symbol.rs provide deterministic encode/decode with extensive unit tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15c.1","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:17.711178940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-qpt5","title":"Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation","description":"# Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation

## GOAL
Accelerate CRC32C and BLAKE3 checksum computation using SIMD instructions.

## BACKGROUND (Extreme Software Optimization)
Checksum computation is on the hot path:
- Every block read/write verifies checksum
- ARC cache hits still verify (trust but verify)
- RaptorQ symbols have checksums

SIMD can provide 4-16x speedup depending on instruction set.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. CRC32C Acceleration
```rust
pub fn crc32c_simd(data: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
    #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
    {
        if is_x86_feature_detected!("sse4.2") {
            return crc32c_sse42(data, seed);
        }
    }
    #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]
    {
        if is_aarch64_feature_detected!("crc") {
            return crc32c_arm_crc(data, seed);
        }
    }
    crc32c_fallback(data, seed)
}
```

### 2. BLAKE3 (Already SIMD)
- blake3 crate already uses SIMD
- Ensure proper feature flags enabled
- Benchmark to confirm

### 3. Batch Checksum
```rust
/// Compute checksums for multiple blocks in parallel
pub fn batch_checksum(blocks: &[&[u8]], algo: ChecksumAlgo) -> Vec<u32> {
    // Use rayon for multi-block parallelism
    // Use SIMD within each block
}
```

### 4. Feature Detection at Runtime
- Detect CPU features once at startup
- Store in global (or thread-local)
- Dispatch to appropriate implementation

## TESTS
1. Unit: SIMD matches fallback results
2. Unit: All supported CPUs tested (via CI matrix)
3. Benchmark: Before/after comparison
4. Property: Random data, SIMD == fallback

## LOGGING
- Feature detection (info): detected SIMD capabilities
- Batch checksum (trace): block count, duration

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Correctness: SIMD == fallback for all inputs
2. Performance: >2x speedup on supported CPUs
3. Graceful fallback on unsupported CPUs
4. No unsafe code (use safe SIMD wrappers)

## NOTES
Use existing crates:
- crc32c crate has SIMD support
- blake3 crate is already optimized
Main work is ensuring proper feature flags and benchmarking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:14.096946312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163955333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163936237Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","perf","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.533583752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.930946133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-tbn","title":"Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration","description":"# Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration

## GOAL
Implement btrfs transaction boundaries integrated with FrankenFS MVCC, bridging native btrfs semantics with our version-chain model.

## BACKGROUND
btrfs transactions are fundamentally different from ext4/JBD2:
- Transactions are tree-level, not journal-level
- Commit creates new tree roots
- Old roots preserved for snapshots
- This naturally maps to MVCC!

The key insight: btrfs transaction = MVCC transaction boundary.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Transaction Handle
```rust
pub struct BtrfsTransaction {
    /// MVCC transaction ID
    txn_id: TxnId,
    /// Snapshot for reads
    snapshot: Snapshot,
    /// Pending tree modifications
    pending_trees: BTreeMap<TreeId, TreeRoot>,
    /// Delayed refs accumulated
    delayed_refs: DelayedRefQueue,
    /// New blocks allocated
    allocated: Vec<BlockNumber>,
    /// Blocks to free on commit
    to_free: Vec<BlockNumber>,
}
```

### 2. Begin/Commit/Abort
```rust
impl BtrfsTransaction {
    pub fn begin(store: &MvccStore, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Self>;
    
    pub fn commit(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<CommitSeq> {
        // 1. Flush delayed refs
        // 2. Update super block with new roots
        // 3. MVCC commit (FCW check)
        // 4. Persist to device
    }
    
    pub fn abort(self) {
        // Free allocated blocks
        // Discard pending changes
    }
}
```

### 3. Tree Root Updates
On commit:
- New tree roots written
- Super block updated with generation and roots
- Checksum tree updated
- Free space cache updated

### 4. Compatibility Mode
For mounting existing btrfs images:
- Read existing transaction log
- Initialize MVCC from latest committed generation
- Subsequent writes use MVCC

## TESTS
1. Unit: Begin/abort discards changes
2. Unit: Begin/commit persists changes
3. Integration: Concurrent transactions with disjoint trees
4. Integration: FCW conflict detection for same tree
5. Recovery: Crash before commit -> rollback

## LOGGING
- Transaction begin (debug): txn_id, generation
- Transaction commit (info): duration, trees modified
- Transaction abort (warn): reason

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ACID semantics preserved
2. Concurrent writers do not corrupt
3. Crash recovery to last committed state
4. MVCC snapshots see consistent trees","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:55.750691013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103301376Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103251292Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs transaction model over MVCC in ffs-btrfs with begin/commit/abort semantics, delayed-ref flush, tree-root staging, FCW conflict coverage, and workspace gates passing. Remaining core on-disk wiring is tracked as broader write-path follow-up.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-1b7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.509360853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:21.021338413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-3sj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.626558426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":30,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This covers 'btrfs transaction parity' (currently ❌). Maps to transaction.c. When complete: flip to ✅ in FEATURE_PARITY.md.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:50Z"},{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BTRFS TRANSACTION MODEL: btrfs transactions group multiple COW tree updates into an atomic unit. Key concepts: (1) transaction handle (acquire on begin, release on end), (2) delayed items (buffered in memory until transaction commit), (3) transaction commit writes all dirty trees atomically. Our MVCC layer maps naturally onto this: MVCC transaction ≈ btrfs transaction.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:59Z"},{"id":110,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"MossyKnoll","text":"Progress update (2026-02-13): implemented  in  with MVCC-backed begin/commit/abort, staged tree-root metadata records, delayed-ref flush before commit, allocation/free bookkeeping, and structured tracing. Updated  row for btrfs transaction parity to 🟡 with remaining gap note.
